intro: Bucket scores higher for developer experience (4/5 vs 3/5). Convert: A/B testing and personalization platform with no-cookie tracking and privacy-first architecture. Bucket: Feature Flags / Remote Config tool for developers. Specializes in Feature Management. Convert fits gradual rollouts to reduce risk. Bucket fits gradual rollouts to reduce risk. On our rubric Convert scores 3/5 for developer experience and 4/5 for transparency, while Bucket scores 4/5 and 4/5. The honest trade-off: Convert's main drawback — Solo developer shipping to production; Bucket's — Solo developer shipping to production. Convert is explicitly not the right pick for solo projects. Bucket is not aimed at solo projects. Another Convert advantage: Kill switches for features. Another Convert friction point: Products with no users yet. Our read on Convert: best for gradual rollouts to reduce risk. Convert offers subscription from $599/mo. On Bucket: best for gradual rollouts to reduce risk. Bucket offers subscription from $125/mo. Convert runs on paid subscription pricing with medium lock-in, Bucket on paid subscription with medium lock-in.
quick_take: Convert is for gradual rollouts to reduce risk; Bucket is for gradual rollouts to reduce risk; decide on developer experience.
verdict_a: Choose Convert if gradual rollouts to reduce risk.
verdict_b: Choose Bucket if gradual rollouts to reduce risk, and if a higher DX score (4/5 vs 3/5 in our rubric) nudges the call.

faq.a: Both tools use paid subscription pricing, so a clean list-price comparison depends on tier details that vary by usage. Check each vendor's current plan page for your expected volume. Neither is structurally cheaper on the model alone.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Convert to Bucket?
faq.a: Expect real effort: Convert is medium lock-in (A/B test data exportable) and Bucket is medium lock-in (Feature flag platform). Migrating between them means rebuilding integrations, re-authoring config, and accepting new coupling. Scope a spike before committing.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Convert or Bucket?
faq.a: Bucket scores higher in our rubric (4/5 vs 3/5 for Convert). Transparency is 4/5 for Bucket and 4/5 for Convert. DX scores are rubric-based, not benchmarks, so evaluate against your own toolchain.

faq.q: Is Bucket a good alternative to Convert?
faq.a: They sit in the same category, so yes — Bucket is a plausible alternative for many Convert use cases. It fits best when gradual rollouts to reduce risk. Skip it if solo projects.
